episodes.title: Why People Double Down Even When They're Wrong
episodes.description:
Why do intelligent, well-meaning people believe one thing and then do the opposite?

In this video, I explore the psychology and neuroscience of cognitive dissonance — the uncomfortable tension that arises when our beliefs, actions, and reality don’t align. From a 1950s doomsday cult that doubled down after a failed prophecy, to everyday examples like addiction, risk-taking, and abandoning our goals, cognitive dissonance is far more common than we realise.

As a psychiatrist, I see this mechanism regularly in clinical practice. It isn’t about stupidity or arrogance. It’s about our brain’s deep need for consistency. We’re often more motivated to protect our identity than to update our beliefs.

I’ll break down:

* The original Leon Festinger study that coined the term
* The brain regions involved (including the anterior cingulate cortex and prefrontal cortex)
* Why we rationalise behaviour instead of changing it
* How motivational interviewing helps reveal (not eliminate) dissonance

Understanding cognitive dissonance won’t make you immune to it. But it can help you spot it in yourself and create the space for meaningful change.

If you’re interested in the science behind mental health and human behaviour, subscribe for new videos every week.

I hope you learn something! Please do let me know what you think!

TIMESTAMPS
0:00 Intro
1:12 doomsday study
3:50 cognitive dissonance
6:57 neuroscience
8:47 motivational interviewing

🎥 Watch next: South Reaction (Addiction): https://youtu.be/MjdRjlqDnWU

Thanks for watching, and love you bye! 🩺🔬  

#explained #neuroscience #doctor #elliott #addiction #motivation

*The information in this video is not intended nor implied to be a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis or treatment. All content, including text, graphics, images, and information, contained in this video is for general information purposes only and does not replace a consultation with your own doctor/health professional *

episodes.title: Doctor Analyzes Dancing For The Devil (The TikTok Dancing Cult is Brainwashing You)
episodes.description:
#doctorreacts #drelliott #tiktok #psychiatrist #mentalhealth

Check out my reaction to Bojack Horseman: https://youtu.be/k6DqqKDm6Lk
It's a Sin reviews: https://youtu.be/KiHx8GHTem8

This Doctor Reacts video is watching the new Netflix documentary called Dancing for the Devil, all about a TikTok cult that recruits dancers and promises them fame and fortune, but even though this cult uses social media, its brainwashing tactics are no different to cults from decades ago, and on this video we unpick what cults are and how they work.

Let me know what you think.

episodes.title: Are Cults Mentally Ill? | ASK A DOCTOR [Real Medical Cases]
episodes.description:
#cults #mentalhealth #askadoctor #psychiatrydoctor #drelliott


Some cults have done some horrible things. As a psychiatry doctor, it's important for me to know what crimes are from mental illness vs. those that are simply from choice. Folie a deux refers to mass shared delusions. Do these violent and bizarre beliefs that come from these cult leaders count as delusions? If group members are therefore psychotic, can they be held responsible for their actions? It's actually a really difficult question to answer but I'll try my best to give it a go on this video.
